metadata.literature_gap_log: (a) Upstream regulators of MOC1 transcription largely unknown beyond the TAB1/MOC3-FON1 axis and the post-translational SLR1/TAD1/TE regulators; transcriptional activators of MOC1 promoter not comprehensively mapped. (b) Full upstream cascade of NGR5 transcription not well characterized — most NGR5 regulation is post-translational (GA-GID1 degradation, N-responsive phosphorylation). (c) Direct transcriptional regulators of the SL biosynthesis genes D27, D10, D17 beyond NGR5/N and auxin/BR are sparsely reported. (d) The rice OsCKX9 regulation by D53 is inferred from its SL-responsiveness; whether D53 directly binds the OsCKX9 promoter has not been fully demonstrated. (e) ABA biosynthesis connection to tillering via NCED1 is inferred from Arabidopsis/pea BRC1 studies; direct rice-specific biochemical validation is limited.

edges.mechanism: SL binds D14 alpha/beta hydrolase, induces conformational change and formation of D14-D3-D53 complex

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of strigolactone signalling in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 was shown to interact with D14 by pull-down assay. Interaction enhanced by GR24 in a dose-dependent manner.
evidence.claim: SL binding activates D14 to form complex with D53

evidence.title: D14-SCFD3-dependent degradation of D53 regulates strigolactone signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: Y2H showed that both D53 and d53 could physically interact with D14 in the presence of GR24.

edges.mechanism: D14 recruits SCF-D3 E3 ligase to ubiquitinate D53 for 26S proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of strigolactone signalling in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 protein levels decreased rapidly upon GR24 treatment in wild-type seedlings. Mutations at Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad could attenuate the SL-induced degradation of D53.
evidence.claim: D14 is essential for SL-induced D53 degradation

edges.mechanism: D3 is the F-box subunit of SCF complex that ubiquitinates D53; required for proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: D14-SCFD3-dependent degradation of D53 regulates strigolactone signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLs induce D53 degradation by the proteasome and abrogate its activity in promoting axillary bud outgrowth, in a D14- and D3-dependent manner.
evidence.claim: D3 (F-box) is required for D53 ubiquitination and degradation

edges.mechanism: D53 binds IPA1 via direct protein-protein interaction and represses IPA1 transcriptional activation activity

evidence.title: IPA1 functions as a downstream transcription factor repressed by D53 in strigolactone signaling in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 physically interacts with IPA1 in vivo and in vitro and suppresses the transcriptional activation activity of IPA1.

edges.mechanism: D53 physically interacts with MOC1 to repress tiller bud outgrowth

evidence.title: Strigolactones and BRC1 directly regulate tillering via D53 and MOC1
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 interacts with MOC1 to repress MOC1 activity in tiller regulation.

edges.mechanism: D53 represses OsCKX9 expression; SL-induced D53 degradation derepresses OsCKX9

evidence.title: Strigolactone promotes cytokinin degradation through transcriptional activation of CYTOKININ OXIDASE/DEHYDROGENASE 9 in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 represses OsCKX9; knockdown of D53 increases OsCKX9 expression.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 directly binds GTAC motif in OsTB1 promoter via SBP domain and activates transcription

evidence.evidence_sentence: IPA1 could directly bind to the promoter of rice TEOSINTE BRANCHED1 via GTAC motif binding to suppress rice tillering.

evidence.title: IPA1 functions as a downstream TF repressed by D53
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPA1 directly binds to the promoter of a negative regulator of tiller bud outgrowth, TEOSINTE BRANCHED1 (OsTB1), to suppress rice tillering.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 directly binds GTAC element in D53 promoter and activates D53 transcription (negative feedback loop with D53 protein repressing IPA1)

evidence.title: IPA1 functions as a downstream TF repressed by D53
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPA1 directly binds D53 promoter GTAC element and activates D53 transcription, forming negative feedback loop.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 binds GTAC core motifs in OsDEP1 promoter, activating panicle morphology gene

evidence.title: Genome-Wide Binding Analysis of IPA1
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPA1 can directly bind to the promoter of DEP1 at different sites with GTAC core motifs.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 directly binds OsLOG promoter; OsLOG is cytokinin activating enzyme

evidence.title: Genome-Wide Binding Analysis of IPA1
evidence.evidence_sentence: EMSA assays confirmed that IPA1 could directly target the promoter or first intron of LOG.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 directly binds OsPIN1b promoter to activate auxin efflux carrier expression

evidence.evidence_sentence: EMSA confirmed IPA1 could directly target PIN1b promoter.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 activates OsHK4 via TGGGCC motif binding with TCP cofactors PCF1/PCF2, completing positive feedback loop

edges.mechanism: OsmiR156 cleaves IPA1/OsSPL14 mRNA via miR156 recognition site in 3' region

edges.mechanism: IPI1 is a RING-type E3 ubiquitin ligase that ubiquitinates IPA1; K48-linked chains in panicle drive degradation, K63 chains in shoot apex are non-proteolytic

evidence.title: Enhanced OsNLP4-OsNiR cascade
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsNLP4 RWP-RK domain binds NRE motif in OsNiR promoter enhancing transcription.

edges.mechanism: Nitrate triggers nuclear retention of NLP4 for transcription activation

evidence.title: Enhanced OsNLP4-OsNiR cascade
evidence.evidence_sentence: Nitrate triggers nuclear retention of NLP4 and transcription activation.

edges.mechanism: OsNiR reduces nitrite to ammonia, enabling N assimilation that supports tiller growth

evidence.title: Enhanced OsNLP4-OsNiR cascade
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsNiR converts nitrite to ammonia; enhanced nitrogen assimilation supports tiller growth.
evidence.claim: OsNiR activates tillering via N assimilation

edges.mechanism: P-sufficient conditions repress SL biosynthesis genes D27, D10, D17; P-deficiency elevates them

evidence.title: Contribution of strigolactones to tiller bud inhibition under phosphate deficiency
evidence.evidence_sentence: Under P-deficient conditions, expression levels of SL biosynthesis genes were elevated; P-deficiency elevates SL content.

edges.mechanism: P deficiency elevates D10/CCD8 expression

evidence.title: SL under phosphate deficiency
evidence.evidence_sentence: P deficiency elevates D10 expression driving SL biosynthesis.

edges.mechanism: P deficiency elevates D17 expression

evidence.title: SL under phosphate deficiency
evidence.evidence_sentence: P deficiency elevates D17 expression.

edges.mechanism: Strigolactone inhibits axillary bud outgrowth via the D14-D53-IPA1-OsTB1 cascade and via OsCKX9-mediated CK degradation; encoded here as direct summary edge (the detailed cascade is also represented)

evidence.title: Inhibition of shoot branching by new terpenoid plant hormones
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLs move to axillary buds and inhibit their outgrowth.

edges.mechanism: IPA1 accumulation (ipa1-1D) reduces tiller number (via OsTB1 activation); summary edge — the detailed IPA1→OsTB1→Tiller cascade is also represented

evidence.title: Regulation of OsSPL14 by OsmiR156
evidence.evidence_sentence: Accumulation of OsSPL14 leads to the formation of ideal plant architecture with decreased tiller number and increased plant height and panicle branches.

edges.mechanism: NPF7.7 nitrate/ammonium transporter promotes effective tillers

evidence.title: NPF7.7 in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: NPF7.7 can increase the inflow of both NO3- and NH4+, thereby promoting the number of effective tillers.

edges.mechanism: OsAAP5 amino acid permease affects cytokinin biosynthesis precursors

evidence.title: OsAAP5 tiller number
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsAAP5 can affect rice tiller number and yield by regulating cytokinin biosynthesis.

edges.mechanism: GS1;2 (glutamine synthetase) promotes CK production via primary N assimilation

evidence.title: GS1;2 in rice tillering
evidence.evidence_sentence: GS1;2 functions in primary assimilation of NH4+ and promotes tiller bud growth by CK production.

edges.mechanism: SLR1 increases tiller number via MOC1 protein stabilization and NGR5 stabilization

evidence.title: SLR1 inhibits MOC1 degradation
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLR1-OE: tiller number was increased; SLR1 protects MOC1 from degradation.

edges.mechanism: GA inhibits tillering via SLR1 and NGR5 degradation; summary edge

evidence.title: Green revolution mutant rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA-deficient and GA-signaling mutants produce more tillers than wild type; sd1 allele increases tillers.

edges.mechanism: OsARF12 activates OsCDKF;2 for cell cycle progression

evidence.title: OsARF12 controls tiller angle
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsARF12 targets OsCDKF;2; redundantly controls tiller angle with OsARF17/25.

edges.mechanism: SL promotes depletion of PIN1 from plasma membrane via endocytosis, reducing auxin transport

evidence.title: Advances in rice auxin
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsPIN1/1b responds to nitric oxide and strigolactone in rice; SL-mediated PIN1 depletion reported in dicots.

edges.mechanism: Decapitation removes apical auxin source, releasing axillary buds from apical dominance

evidence.title: Sugar demand initial regulator of apical dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: Decapitation removes apical auxin source; rapid sugar flux precedes auxin depletion at buds.

edges.mechanism: Apical auxin maintains bud dormancy via cytokinin suppression, SL biosynthesis activation, and BRC1/OsTB1 activation; summary edge

evidence.title: Inhibition of shoot branching by SLs
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxins inhibit axillary bud outgrowth and do not migrate to axillary buds.

edges.mechanism: miR444a OE reduces tillers via OsMADS57 mRNA cleavage (summary edge)

evidence.title: OsMADS57-OsTB1 interaction
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsMIR444a-overexpressing lines: suppressed OsMADS57 expression, fewer tillers.

edges.mechanism: SL signaling indirectly activates OsTB1 via D53 degradation derepressing IPA1; OsTB1 expression increases under SL

evidence.title: FC1 works downstream of SL
evidence.evidence_sentence: FC1 expression was not significantly affected by GR24 treatment directly — SL acts post-transcriptionally on FC1 function; indirect activation through IPA1 cascade.

edges.mechanism: High N represses OsTB1 via NGR5-PRC2 H3K27me3 deposition

evidence.title: Enhanced sustainable green revolution yield
evidence.evidence_sentence: Nitrogen fertilizer causes the downregulation of OsTB1 to promote tiller formation.

edges.mechanism: High N represses OsTCP19 via LBD37/39 induction

evidence.title: Genomic basis of adaptation to soil nitrogen
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsTCP19 expression is induced at low nitrogen levels but is repressed at higher concentrations.

edges.mechanism: N sufficiency represses SL biosynthesis genes D10, D17, D27

evidence.title: Strigolactones and nitrogen in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D10, D17, D27; high N represses them.

edges.mechanism: N sufficiency represses D17/HTD1 SL biosynthesis

evidence.title: Strigolactones and nitrogen in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D17; high N represses.

edges.mechanism: N sufficiency represses D27 SL biosynthesis

evidence.title: Strigolactones and nitrogen in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D27; high N represses.

edges.mechanism: Light signal entrains OsCCA1 expression (canonical circadian clock input)

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 shows morning-peak circadian expression entrained by light, and integrates sugar signaling in tiller regulation.

edges.mechanism: Photoperiod regulates OsPRR1 evening expression

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsPRR1 evening-peak expression is photoperiod-dependent in rice clock.

edges.mechanism: OsTAR2 is TAA/TAR aminotransferase; first step of IAA biosynthesis from tryptophan to IPA

evidence.title: Advances in rice auxin
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsTAR2 catalyzes the first step of tryptophan-dependent IAA biosynthesis in rice.

edges.mechanism: OsYUCCA enzymes catalyze rate-limiting step of IAA biosynthesis (IPA to IAA)

evidence.title: Advances in rice auxin
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsYUCCAs are rate-limiting enzymes in auxin synthesis; multiple members contribute to tiller angle and architecture.

edges.mechanism: Physical interaction between OsTB1 and OsMADS57 relieves OsMADS57 repression on D14

evidence.title: OsMADS57-OsTB1 interaction
evidence.evidence_sentence: Interaction of OsMADS57 with OsTB1 reduced OsMADS57 inhibition of D14 transcription.

edges.mechanism: MOC1 positively affects axillary meristem gene network including LAX1

evidence.title: LAX PANICLE1 regulation
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC1 works together with LAX1 in axillary meristem formation; lax1 moc1 double mutant shows enhanced AM suppression.

edges.mechanism: SLs exuded from roots induce hyphal branching of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi

evidence.title: Branching factor produced by AM fungi host plants
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLs exudated from roots induce hyphal branching of AM fungi.

edges.mechanism: OsPRR1-OE reduces tillers (summary of clock effect via OsCCA1 repression)

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsPRR1-OE: reduced tiller numbers.

edges.mechanism: OsCCA1 represses tillering via OsTB1/D14/IPA1 activation

evidence.title: Rice Circadian Clock Regulates Tiller Growth
evidence.evidence_sentence: oscca1 mutant shows more tillers and delayed flowering; OsCCA1-OE fewer tillers.

edges.mechanism: TAD1 inhibits tillering via MOC1 degradation (summary)

evidence.title: Degradation of MONOCULM 1 by APC/CTAD1
evidence.evidence_sentence: tad1 mutant showed increased tiller number; TAD1-OX reduced tiller number.

edges.mechanism: TE inhibits tillering via MOC1 degradation (summary)

evidence.title: APC/CTE controls tillering
evidence.evidence_sentence: te mutant displays drastically increased tiller number.

edges.mechanism: D53 accumulation promotes tillering (via IPA1 repression and SL signaling block); summary

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of SL signalling
evidence.evidence_sentence: d53 mutant shows ~3x wild-type tiller number due to D53 accumulation.

edges.mechanism: OsCKX2 depletes CK reducing tillering (summary)

evidence.title: Cytokinin oxidase regulates rice grain production
evidence.evidence_sentence: Reduced OsCKX2 expression causes cytokinin accumulation and increased tillers; Gn1a reduced-function allele is high-tillering.

edges.mechanism: OsLAX1 is a synonym for LAX1 (LAX PANICLE1) in rice

evidence.title: LAX and SPA major regulators
evidence.evidence_sentence: LAX1 (also called OsLAX1 or LAX PANICLE1) regulates axillary meristem formation.

edges.mechanism: Sucrose synthesis generates trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P) signaling molecule

evidence.title: Branching sucrose and SL signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: Mounting evidence indicates that sucrose effects on branching are mediated by trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P).

edges.mechanism: T6P signaling represses BRC1/OsTB1 expression

evidence.title: Sucrose SL signaling bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sucrose/T6P signaling partially represses PsBRC1 (OsTB1 ortholog) expression in peripheral buds.

edges.mechanism: NRT1.1B-dependent phosphorylation promotes NGR5 stability (nitrate-sensing-to-chromatin)

evidence.title: NRT1.1B natural variation
evidence.evidence_sentence: NRT1.1B natural variation in indica enhances N uptake signaling that converges on NGR5 activity.

edges.mechanism: GID1 is GA receptor; GA-GID1 complex targets SLR1 for SCF-GID2-mediated proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: GID1 GA receptor rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: GID1 is a soluble GA receptor; GA-GID1 complex promotes SLR1 interaction with F-box SCF-GID2 for degradation.

edges.mechanism: GA-GID1 complex directly targets NGR5 for proteasomal degradation

evidence.title: Green revolution yield chromatin modulation
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA receptor GID1 targets NGR5 for proteolysis by the 26S proteasome.

edges.mechanism: OsGA20ox2 (SD1) catalyzes multiple oxidation steps in GA biosynthesis

evidence.title: Green revolution mutant rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: sd1 allele is a null mutation in GA20ox2; sd1 plants have reduced GA levels.

edges.mechanism: CK signaling induces type-A OsRRs as primary response genes (negative feedback on CK signaling)

evidence.title: Rice cytokinin signaling genes
evidence.evidence_sentence: Most type-A OsRR genes rapidly upregulated by cytokinin (peak 30-60 min).

edges.mechanism: Type-A OsRRs provide negative feedback on CK signaling

evidence.title: Rice cytokinin signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: Type-A OsRRs act as negative regulators of CK signaling (classical feedback).

edges.mechanism: D53 contains EAR motifs and recruits TOPLESS/TPR co-repressors for transcriptional silencing

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of SL signalling
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 contains three putative EAR motifs and interacts with TOPLESS-RELATED PROTEINS (TPR1, TPR2, TPR3).

edges.mechanism: TPL co-repressor recruited by D53 represses IPA1-bound target genes

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of SL signalling
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53-TPR interaction likely represses IPA1 transcriptional activation by delivering TPR co-repressors to IPA1-bound loci.

edges.mechanism: BR signaling activates DLT expression/function in tiller promotion

evidence.title: DLT in BR signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: DLT plays positive roles in brassinosteroid signaling in rice; BR pathway activates DLT.

edges.mechanism: SL-bound D14 directly binds D3 F-box protein to form D14-D3 complex required for D53 recruitment and ubiquitination; D3 C-terminal alpha-helix switches to engaged form upon binding

evidence.title: Destabilization of strigolactone receptor DWARF14 by binding of ligand and E3-ligase signaling effector DWARF3
evidence.evidence_sentence: GR24 induces a direct interaction between D14 and D3 measured by AlphaScreen assay; D14 surface residues required for D14-D3 interaction are clustered at the open lid domain surrounding the entrance of the ligand binding pocket.

edges.mechanism: NGR5 binds IPA1/OsSPL14 promoter and recruits PRC2 complex to deposit H3K27me3 mark; ChIP-qPCR direct binding confirmed; nitrogen dose-dependent

evidence.title: Enhanced sustainable green revolution yield via nitrogen-responsive chromatin modulation in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: ChIP-qPCR analysis showed that NGR5 can directly bind to the promoter region of D14 and SPL14, and the binding strength increased with increasing nitrogen concentration.

edges.mechanism: Photosynthesis-derived sucrose activates NGR5 expression at the shoot base, providing carbon-nitrogen integration for tiller bud elongation

evidence.title: Photosynthesis regulates tillering bud elongation and nitrogen-use efficiency via sugar-induced NGR5 in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: The expression of nitrogen-mediated tiller growth response 5 (NGR5) was remarkably decreased in shoot base of dtn1-2, which can be activated by sucrose treatment.

edges.mechanism: DTN1 (Decreased Tiller Number 1) encodes fructose-1,6-bisphosphate aldolase and is required for photosynthesis and sucrose accumulation in shoot base / axillary buds

evidence.title: Photosynthesis regulates tillering bud elongation and nitrogen-use efficiency via sugar-induced NGR5 in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Deficiency of DTN1 results in reduced photosynthetic rate and decreased contents of sucrose and other sugars in both leaves and axillary buds.

edges.mechanism: OsVIL2 chromatin-interacting factor binds OsTB1 promoter and represses OsTB1 expression via chromatin modification, releasing axillary bud outgrowth

evidence.title: Chromatin Interacting Factor OsVIL2 Is Required for Outgrowth of Axillary Buds in Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Expression of OsTB1 was increased in osvil2 mutants. ChIP assays showed that OsVIL2 binds to the promoter region of OsTB1 in wild-type rice; tiller number of double mutant osvil2 ostb1 was similar to that of ostb1 (osvil2 epistatic to ostb1).

edges.mechanism: OsGATA8 transcription factor represses OsTCP19 (negative regulator of tillering), promoting productive tiller formation under N-replete conditions

evidence.title: The elite haplotype OsGATA8-H coordinates nitrogen uptake and productive tiller formation in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsGATA8 promotes tiller formation by repressing the transcription of OsTCP19, a negative modulator of tillering.

edges.mechanism: OsGATA8 directly represses OsAMT3.2 ammonium transporter; the OsGATA8-H haplotype balances N uptake with tiller productivity

evidence.title: The elite haplotype OsGATA8-H coordinates nitrogen uptake and productive tiller formation in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsGATA8 negatively regulates nitrogen uptake by repressing transcription of the ammonium transporter gene OsAMT3.2.

edges.mechanism: OsGATA8-H haplotype increases productive tiller proportion by simultaneously gating N uptake (via OsAMT3.2 repression) and de-repressing tiller via OsTCP19 repression

evidence.title: The elite haplotype OsGATA8-H coordinates nitrogen uptake and productive tiller formation in rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: The OsGATA8-H haplotype is identified as a high-NUE haplotype with enhanced nitrogen uptake and a higher proportion of productive tillers.

edges.mechanism: Os1900 (one of five rice MAX1-like paralogs) catalyzes carlactone-to-carlactonoic acid conversion together with Os5100; Os1900 is the N-fertilization-responsive paralog

evidence.title: Fertilization controls tiller numbers via transcriptional regulation of a MAX1-like gene in rice cultivation
evidence.evidence_sentence: Os1900 together with another MAX1-like gene, Os5100, play a critical role in controlling the conversion of carlactone into carlactonoic acid during strigolactone biosynthesis and tillering in rice.

edges.mechanism: Fertilization (N supply) transcriptionally upregulates Os1900 promoter; promoter mutations that disrupt N-induction increase tiller numbers under low fertilizer

evidence.title: Fertilization controls tiller numbers via transcriptional regulation of a MAX1-like gene in rice cultivation
evidence.evidence_sentence: Fertilization controls tiller number in rice through transcriptional regulation of Os1900, and a few promoter mutations alone can increase tiller numbers and grain yields even under minor-fertilizer conditions.

edges.mechanism: MOC1 physically interacts with MOC3 (OsWUS/TAB1) and acts as a transcriptional co-activator; MOC1 cannot bind FON1 promoter alone but cooperates with MOC3 binding

evidence.title: Tiller Bud Formation Regulators MOC1 and MOC3 Cooperatively Promote Tiller Bud Outgrowth by Activating FON1 Expression in Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC3 is able to directly bind the promoter of FON1 and subsequently activate FON1 expression, while MOC1 functions as a co-activator of MOC3 but could not directly bind the FON1 promoter.
evidence.claim: MOC1 cooperates with MOC3 as co-activator

edges.mechanism: MOC3 (TAB1/OsWUS) is required for axillary meristem initiation in cooperation with MOC1; loss of MOC3 prevents tiller formation (summary edge)

evidence.title: Tiller Bud Formation Regulators MOC1 and MOC3 Cooperatively Promote Tiller Bud Outgrowth by Activating FON1 Expression in Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: MOC1 and MOC3 cooperatively promote tiller bud outgrowth by activating FON1 expression in rice.

edges.mechanism: T20 encodes ζ-carotene isomerase (Z-ISO) acting upstream of D27 in the carotenoid pathway; required for SL precursor supply

evidence.title: ζ-Carotene Isomerase Suppresses Tillering in Rice through the Coordinated Biosynthesis of Strigolactone and Abscisic Acid
evidence.evidence_sentence: The T20 gene encodes a chloroplast ζ-carotene isomerase (Z-ISO), which is involved in the biosynthesis of carotenoids and their metabolites, strigolactone (SL) and abscisic acid (ABA).

edges.mechanism: T20/Z-ISO supplies carotenoid precursors for ABA biosynthesis (parallel to SL biosynthesis arm)

evidence.title: ζ-Carotene Isomerase Suppresses Tillering in Rice through the Coordinated Biosynthesis of Strigolactone and Abscisic Acid
evidence.evidence_sentence: T20 encodes Z-ISO involved in the biosynthesis of carotenoids and their metabolites, strigolactone (SL) and abscisic acid (ABA).

edges.mechanism: rac-GR24 (synthetic SL) induces OsHOX12 expression in shoot base; OsHOX12 then transactivates OsNCED1 to drive ABA biosynthesis

evidence.title: ζ-Carotene Isomerase Suppresses Tillering in Rice through the Coordinated Biosynthesis of Strigolactone and Abscisic Acid
evidence.evidence_sentence: rac-GR24 stimulated expression of OsNCED1 through induction of OsHOX12, promoting ABA biosynthesis in shoot base.

edges.mechanism: OsHOX12 homeodomain TF directly induces OsNCED1 expression to promote ABA biosynthesis (SL → OsHOX12 → NCED1 → ABA)

evidence.title: ζ-Carotene Isomerase Suppresses Tillering in Rice through the Coordinated Biosynthesis of Strigolactone and Abscisic Acid
evidence.evidence_sentence: rac-GR24 stimulated expression of OsNCED1 through induction of OsHOX12, promoting ABA biosynthesis in shoot base.

edges.mechanism: ABA represses SL biosynthesis genes (D10, D17, D27); ABA biosynthetic mutants show elevated SL biosynthesis (negative feedback from ABA to SL)

evidence.title: ζ-Carotene Isomerase Suppresses Tillering in Rice through the Coordinated Biosynthesis of Strigolactone and Abscisic Acid
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABA treatment significantly repressed SL biosynthesis and ABA biosynthetic mutants displayed elevated SL biosynthesis.

edges.mechanism: OsHK3/OsHK5 are paralogous CK histidine kinase receptors with partial redundancy with OsHK4; phosphorylate OsAHP1/2 in cytokinin signalling

evidence.title: The cytokinin receptor OHK4/OsHK4 regulates inflorescence architecture in rice via an IDEAL PLANT ARCHITECTURE1/WEALTHY FARMER'S PANICLE-mediated positive feedback circuit
evidence.evidence_sentence: Similar to its homologs OHK3/OsHK5 and OHK5/OsHK6, knockout of OHK4 resulted in shorter panicles with reduced numbers of branches and grains due to the reduction in SAM (shoot apical meristem) and IM (inflorescence meristem) activity.

edges.mechanism: Cytokinin binds OsHK3 CHASE domain (paralogous to OsHK4) activating histidine kinase phosphorelay

evidence.title: Characterization of Genes Involved in Cytokinin Signaling and Metabolism from Rice
evidence.evidence_sentence: Rice has 5 histidine kinase (HK) genes (OsHK1-4, OsHKL1) that function as cytokinin-response histidine protein kinases; CK binds CHASE-domain receptors.

edges.mechanism: OsDRM1 (Dormancy-Associated Gene 1) is a downstream marker of bud dormancy in rice; predominantly expressed in dormant axillary buds where OsTB1/FC1 is high

evidence.title: Dormancy-Associated Gene 1 (OsDRM1) as an axillary bud dormancy marker
evidence.evidence_sentence: OsDRM1 is predominantly expressed in dormant axillary buds, while it is weakly expressed in growing buds, indicating that OsDRM1 gene can be used as a molecular marker for bud dormancy in rice.
evidence.claim: OsTB1-driven dormancy correlates with OsDRM1 expression

edges.mechanism: OsDRM1 OE delays axillary bud outgrowth by suppressing cell-cycle progression; acts as a growth repressor by modulating auxin response

evidence.title: Dormancy-Associated Gene 1 (OsDRM1) as an axillary bud dormancy marker
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of OsDRM1 in transgenic plants delayed axillary bud outgrowth by suppressing cell division within the buds.

edges.mechanism: Os1900 is one of the five rice OsMAX1 paralogs (Os900, Os1400, Os1500, Os1900, Os5100); the OsMAX1 generic node aggregates this family

evidence.title: Rice cytochrome P450 MAX1 homologs catalyze distinct steps in strigolactone biosynthesis
evidence.evidence_sentence: Rice has five MAX1-like genes (Os900, Os1400, Os1500, Os1900, Os5100) catalysing distinct steps in strigolactone biosynthesis.
